name: Waterfall and Spring of Urederra from Baquedano
name_original: Cascada y Nacedero del Urederra desde Baquedano
region_intro: Jogging routes around Améscoa Baja traverse a landscape characterized by natural springs, waterfalls, and varied forest terrain. The region features a mix of moderate and more challenging running paths, often leading through areas with significant natural beauty. Elevation changes are common, with some routes offering substantial climbs. Best jogging routes around Améscoa Baja The most popular jogging route is Waterfall and Spring of Urederra from Baquedano, a 3.8 miles (6.1 km) trail that takes 42 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the natural beauty of the Urederra spring and its waterfalls. Another top favourite among local runners is San Miguel Hermitage loop from Allín / Allin, a difficult 14.0 miles (22.6 km) path. This running route features significant elevation gain through the local hills and forests. Local runners also love the Santiago de Lokiz Hermitage – Santiago Rock loop from Allín / Allin, a 23.9 miles (38.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain including rocky sections and woodlands, often completed in about 5 hours 53 minutes. Jogging around Améscoa Baja is defined by natural springs, waterfalls, and forested hills. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult running, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Améscoa Baja are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.0 stars from 1 review. More than 250 runners have used komoot to explore Améscoa Baja's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the typical length and difficulty of running trails in Améscoa Baja?
A: The running routes in Améscoa Baja vary significantly in length and difficulty. You'll find moderate trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971569888/waterfall-and-spring-of-urederra-from-baquedano" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall and Spring of Urederra from Baquedano&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.8 miles (6.1 km), as well as more challenging options such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24570016" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Miguel Hermitage loop from Allín / Allin&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 14.0 miles (22.6 km) path with significant elevation gain. Overall, there are 8 moderate and 17 difficult routes available.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Améscoa Baja?
A: Améscoa Baja's terrain is characterized by elevation changes and varied landscapes, meaning most routes are either moderate or difficult. There are no routes specifically classified as 'easy' in the current selection, but moderate options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971569888/waterfall-and-spring-of-urederra-from-baquedano" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall and Spring of Urederra from Baquedano&lt;/a&gt; offer a good starting point for those comfortable with some inclines.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see while running in Améscoa Baja?
A: Jogging routes in Améscoa Baja often traverse landscapes rich in natural beauty. You can expect to encounter natural springs, waterfalls, and varied forest terrain. Notable natural attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1390836" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Urederra River&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3065525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portupekoleze Arch&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available in the region?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Améscoa Baja are designed as loops. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24570016" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Miguel Hermitage loop from Allín / Allin&lt;/a&gt; and the extensive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31881199" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santiago de Lokiz Hermitage – Santiago Rock loop from Allín / Allin&lt;/a&gt;, which offers varied terrain over a longer distance.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or points of interest near the running trails?
A: Beyond the natural springs and waterfalls, you can discover several interesting landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3006968" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ubaba Viewpoint (Pilatos Balcony)&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic views, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3426453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bargagain Summit and Cross&lt;/a&gt; provides a rewarding destination. For historical interest, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6195223" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iranzu Monastery&lt;/a&gt; is also nearby.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Améscoa Baja?
A: The running routes in Améscoa Baja are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.0 stars from 1 review. Over 250 runners have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the natural beauty of the Urederra spring and the challenging yet rewarding climbs through the local hills and forests.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer good viewpoints?
A: Yes, several routes provide excellent viewpoints. While specific routes aren't detailed for viewpoints, the region's hilly and forested nature suggests many opportunities for scenic vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3006968" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ubaba Viewpoint (Pilatos Balcony)&lt;/a&gt; is a notable highlight that can be incorporated into or accessed from various trails, offering expansive views of the landscape.

Q: How many running routes are available in Améscoa Baja?
A: There are over 25 dedicated running routes available for exploration in Améscoa Baja, catering to various fitness levels with a mix of moderate and difficult trails.

Q: Can I find trails suitable for a longer, more challenging run?
A: Absolutely. Améscoa Baja is well-suited for longer, more challenging runs. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31881199" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santiago de Lokiz Hermitage – Santiago Rock loop from Allín / Allin&lt;/a&gt;, at 23.9 miles (38.5 km) with significant elevation, are perfect for experienced runners seeking a demanding workout amidst varied terrain.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by caves or unique geological formations?
A: Yes, the region features interesting geological formations. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3006763" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de Los Cristinos&lt;/a&gt; is a notable cave in the area, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3065525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portupekoleze Arch&lt;/a&gt; is a unique natural monument that runners might encounter or visit nearby.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Améscoa Baja?
A: Given the natural springs, waterfalls, and forested terrain, spring and autumn are generally ideal for jogging in Améscoa Baja. The weather is typically milder, and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summer can also be pleasant, especially in the mornings or evenings, though some routes might be more exposed to sun. Winters can be cold, and higher elevation trails may be affected by snow or ice.

Q: Are there any running routes that start or end near a town or village?
A: Many routes are accessible from local villages. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24570016" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Miguel Hermitage loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31881199" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santiago de Lokiz Hermitage – Santiago Rock loop&lt;/a&gt; both start from Allín / Allin, providing convenient access to the trails.
tips: One of the several waterfalls on the Urredera riverbed and an unmissable place for any nature lover. The environment and landscape that surround it are wonderful and perfectly complement this beautiful waterfall.
A beautiful and picturesque place with a lot of tranquility. Arriving here by the path that runs along the river is a pleasure for all lovers of nature, forest and water in the same place!
